revision.text:
[[en:Ebetsu shrine]][[fr:Ebetsu-jinja]][[ja:江別神社]] {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|Ebetsu Jinja|江別神社|qq=}} | image = File:Ebetsu Shrine.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|43.11055556|N|141.55416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Emperor Taishō|qid=Q157052|1=Emperor Taishō}} | founded_by = | established = 1915 | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Ebetsu, Hokkaidō|Ebetsu]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style ={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Ebetsu|qid=Q496681|1=Ebetsu}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ebetsu Jinja'''|江別神社|qq=}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in [[:en:Ebetsu, Hokkaidō|Ebetsu]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was built in honour of the [[:en:Taishō Emperor|Taishō Emperor]] in 1915 and is modelled on the [[shinmei-zukuri]] style. Within the shrine is enshrined [[Amaterasu]].{{cite web |url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01027.html |title=Ebetsu Jinja |publisher=Hokkaidō Jinja-chō |accessdate=22 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091231042232/http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01027.html |archive-date=31 December 2009 |url-status=dead |qq=}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ebetsu Shrine | image = [[File:Ebetsu Shrine.JPG|270px|Ebetsu Shrine]]
Torii gate and Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|6|38.27|N|141|33|15.19|E|qq=}}|qq=}} | location = 1-1 Hagigaoka, {{ill|Ebetsu|en|Ebetsu|qid=Q496681|lt=Ebetsu|1=Ebetsu}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43|6|38.27|N|141|33|15.19|E|display=title,inline|qq=}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]]
[[Ōkuninushi]]
{{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|qid=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa|1=Katō Kiyomasa}} | Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] | founded = {{ill|1885|en|1885|qid=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|September 9|en|September 9|qid=Q2856|lt=September 9|1=September 9}} | ceremony = | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=6|lat_sec=38.2|lon_deg=141|lon_min=33|lon_sec=15.1|label=Ebetsu Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg|qq=}} }} '''Ebetsu Shrine''' (江別神社, Ebetsu Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ebetsu|en|Ebetsu|qid=Q496681|lt=Ebetsu|1=Ebetsu}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine enshrines [[Amaterasu]], [[Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|qid=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa|1=Katō Kiyomasa}}. == History == Ebetsu Shrine was established in {{ill|1885|en|1885|qid=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) by {{ill|Tondenhei|en|Tondenhei|qid=Q11465766|lt=Tondenhei|1=Tondenhei}} (pioneering soldiers) who had immigrated from {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. They enshrined Katō Kiyomasa as their guardian deity and named the shrine "Asukayama Shrine" in Asukayama, Midorimachi, Ebetsu. In {{ill|1891|en|1891|qid=Q7837|lt=1891|1=1891}} (Meiji 24), Ōkuninushi was enshrined from [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]. On August 25, 1891, the shrine was relocated to its current site in Hagigaoka near {{ill|Ebetsu Station|en|Ebetsu Station|qid=Q872626|lt=Ebetsu Station|1=Ebetsu Station}} and renamed "Ebetsu Shrine"{{Sfn|Fujikura|2007|p=39|qq=}}. In {{ill|1915|en|1915|qid=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taisho 4), as a commemorative project for the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|qid=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō|1=Emperor Taishō}}, the shrine building and shrine office were constructed, and [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the main deity from [[Ise Grand Shrine]]. The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine on June 30, {{ill|1917|en|1917|qid=Q2092|lt=1917|1=1917}} (Taisho 6), and was elevated to a gōsha on May 12, {{ill|1926|en|1926|qid=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} (Taisho 15). In {{ill|1984|en|1984|qid=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}} (Showa 59), the current shrine building was constructed to commemorate the shrine's centennial. == References == == Further Reading == *{{cite book|title=Ebetsu Hyakuwa Ge Your Hometown, Scenic Memories|author=Tetsuo Fujikura|series=Yubeotsu Sensho|date=2007-09-22|publisher=Yubeotsu Shobo|ref={{SfnRef|Fujikura|2007|qq=}}|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://park19.wakwak.com/~e-jinjya/ Official Website] * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01027.html Ebetsu Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s in Hokkaido|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} ==See also== * {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{in lang|ja|qq=}} [http://park19.wakwak.com/~e-jinjya/ Ebetsu Jinja homepage] {{Shinto shrine|qq=}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub|qq=}} [[en:Ebetsu shrine]] [[fr:Ebetsu-jinja]] [[ja:江別神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3046830}} [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in 1915]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
